commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the Ollama stream converter and tests so replayed assistant tool calls and tool results retain matching OpenAI/Ollama-compatible IDs.

Reproducibility: yes. for the converter-level failure from source inspection: current main drops assistant tool-call IDs and tool result IDs during Ollama message conversion. I did not establish a live provider reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor-provided real behavior proof, not an automation-safe code repair.

target.title: fix(ollama): preserve replay tool call ids
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve assistant tool call IDs when converting replayed tool calls to Ollama/OpenAI-compatible payloads.
- Preserve tool result `tool_call_id` and include OpenAI-compatible `name` alongside existing `tool_name`.
- Update Ollama stream replay unit coverage to assert IDs survive tool-call/tool-result round trips.

## Why
OpenRouter Anthropic tool-loop follow-up requests can fail when prior assistant tool calls and tool results are replayed without matching tool call IDs. The production failure showed HTTP 500s after an Anthropic model used a tool, while plain replies succeeded.

## Tests
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts -t "OpenRouter"`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts`
target.author: ronchak
